    Vadim Biryukov

    About Me

    I am a Computer Science Instructor at Department of Engineering, Computing and Mathematical Sciences (ECaMS) at Lewis University. I am a PhD candidate in the Department of Computer Science at the Illinois Institute of Technology where I work closely with Prof. Xian-He Sun at the Gnosis Research Center. My research is in system memory architecture, memory hierarchy optimization for computer systems.

    Research Interests

    • System memory architecture
    • Memory hierarchy optimization
    • Hardware prefetchers
    • Computing systems
    • Large Language Models efficiency

    Publications

    Zion: A Comprehensive, Adaptive, and Lightweight Hardware Prefetcher

    Authors: Vadim Biryukov, Xiaoyang Lu, Zirui Liu, Kaixiong Zhou, Xian-He Sun
    Design, Automation and Test in Europe Conference, DATE 2026

    Automated IOT-based performance assessments through activity recognition and semantic evaluation in Smart Learning Environments

    Authors: Gina Martinez, Jason Perry, Vadim Biryukov
    International Conference on Control, Automation and Diagnosis ICCAD 2024
